grpo_model.summary: Remove leading and trailing whitespace from a mutable string buffer.

original_model.summary: This function trims leading and trailing whitespace characters from a mutable C string in place. It first calculates the string length, then advances a start index past all leading whitespace, and reduces an end index before all trailing whitespace. If any leading whitespace was removed, it shifts the remaining substring to the front of the buffer using memcpy. Finally, it null-terminates the string after the trimmed content.

grpo_model.summary: Parse a tiny configuration format from stdin, validate a few typed fields, and report normalized status lines.

original_model.summary: The main function reads up to 16 lines from standard input, each expected to be a key-value pair string. It trims each line, splits it into a Pair structure, and stores it in an array. Then it searches this array for keys "mode", "limit", and "flags". It validates the "mode" key by comparing its value to a constant string. It parses "limit" as a 16-bit unsigned integer and checks if it is between 10 and 99 inclusive. It parses "flags" as an 8-bit hexadecimal value. Finally, it prints the count of items read, validation results for each key, the parsed limit and flags if valid, and a final ready status indicating if all validations passed.
